title: Ruling Class Clowns
content: By the end of the 19th-century in the US, it was clear to all interested observers that market power did not automatically or even easily translate into political capital and cultural authority. That is why, unlike the oligarchs of our time, then, certain Gilded Age capitalists learned to become a ruling class.

creator: James Livingston
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Missing Ingredient for European Tech Sovereignty
content: Europe’s dependence on US technology companies is both an economic challenge and a strategic vulnerability. The European Union’s new Tech Sovereignty Package is a step in the right direction, but without equally ambitious efforts to curb market concentration, it is bound to fall short.
snippet: Europe’s dependence on US technology companies is both an economic challenge and a strategic vulnerability. The European Union’s new Tech Sovereignty Package is a step in the right direction, but
creator: Giorgos Verdi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: In Praise of the World Cup’s Little Guys
content: In a world of great-power rivalry and hyper-scaling tech behemoths, you would be forgiven for thinking that the bulky bully always has the edge. But as this year's World Cup has shown repeatedly, you would be wrong.
snippet: In a world of great-power rivalry and hyper-scaling tech behemoths, you would be forgiven for thinking that the bulky bully always has the edge. But as this year's World Cup has shown repeatedly, you
creator: Andrés Velasco
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Inside Ukraine’s Game-Changing Drone Industry
content: In recent months, Ukrainian drones have successfully struck dozens of sites deep inside Russia, targeting the oil industry, weapons plants, and logistics hubs. But while Ukraine has developed a world-leading military technology to defend itself from a much larger aggressor, drones will not be enough to end the war.

creator: Yuriy Gorodnichenko
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Just Say No to Remote Work
content: Even during the immediate post-pandemic period, when evidence on the labor-market effects of remote work was scant, there was a strong case to be made for going into the office, especially for younger workers. And now, multiple new studies have vindicated this intuition.
snippet: Even during the immediate post-pandemic period, when evidence on the labor-market effects of remote work was scant, there was a strong case to be made for going into the office, especially for younger
creator: Michael R. Strain
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: There Is Only One Acceptable Path for Designer Babies
content: Genetic-engineering tools with which to design one's progeny already exist, and they are being made even more potent with AI—at least for those who can afford such services. In the absence of policy interventions, we will be hurtling toward a tiered society in which economic privileges become biological advantages.

creator: Daron Acemoglu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Democratic Turkey NATO Needs
content: At NATO's upcoming summit in Ankara, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan will seek to portray his country as stable, confident, and essential to Western security. But only the last one is true, which is why the restoration of democracy in Turkey is more than a domestic concern.
snippet: At NATO's upcoming summit in Ankara, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan will seek to portray his country as stable, confident, and essential to Western security. But only the last one is true,
creator: Özgür Özel
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The World Cup as It Should Be
content: While Donald Trump was widely expected to lord over this year’s World Cup, which the United States is co-hosting with Mexico and Canada, it has turned out not to be his scene. National teams populated with immigrants, and small-town America’s embrace of the event, go against everything Trump’s MAGA movement stands for.
snippet: While Donald Trump was widely expected to lord over this year’s World Cup, which the United States is co-hosting with Mexico and Canada, it has turned out not to be his scene. National teams populated
creator: Ian Buruma
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real Threat to NATO
content: The greatest challenge to NATO comes not from its adversaries but from within. As its leaders gather in Ankara on July 7-8, they should focus less on military capabilities than on reinvigorating the alliance's founding strategic philosophy, centered on democratic legitimacy, human rights, and the rule of law.
snippet: The greatest challenge to NATO comes not from its adversaries but from within. As its leaders gather in Ankara on July 7-8, they should focus less on military capabilities than on reinvigorating the
creator: Ahmet Davutoğlu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How the EU Can Lead the Rest of the West
content: While Europe is not just another middle power, nor is it a great power with sufficient economic, military, and geopolitical clout to dictate terms on the global stage. Instead, Europe is the ideal candidate to convene and lead a group of like-minded middle powers, not as a US-style hegemon, but as a first among equals.

creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Safe Are Today’s Blockbuster Tech Stocks?
content: Today’s wave of mega-IPOs has unleashed a wave of analogies to past booms. While no analogy is perfect, an especially apposite—and sobering—case may be what followed Nippon Telegraph and Telephone’s IPO in February 1987.
snippet: Today’s wave of mega-IPOs has unleashed a wave of analogies to past booms. While no analogy is perfect, an especially apposite—and sobering—case may be what followed Nippon Telegraph and Telephone’s
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s AI Dolce Vita?
content: Glacial growth, aging welfare states, and a shortage of centrist leaders suggest Europe faces a major debt crisis in the years ahead. But its emphasis on leisure over work could become a decisive advantage, positioning the continent as the leading model for thriving in an age of AI-driven abundance.

creator: Kenneth Rogoff
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Putin Escalation Risk
content: If Ukraine can continue to disrupt Crimea, strike more targets in the heart of Russia, frustrate Russia’s frontline forces, and pile more pressure on Russia’s hobbled economy, President Vladimir Putin will feel increasingly cornered. The temptation to escape his predicament through a game-changing attack will grow stronger.

creator: Ian Bremmer
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe’s New German Question
content: Without a EU-level framework capable of turning resources into scale, the bloc’s defense capabilities will remain limited, no matter how much member countries spends. If Germany, in particular, insists on going it alone, Europe could remain fully dependent on the US for key assets.

creator: Marco Buti
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From the American Revolution to Universal Suffrage
content: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power, tame corruption, and ensure that imperial rule remained compatible with liberty.
snippet: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power,
creator: Danielle Allen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: 250 Years of American State Capitalism
content: While the prevailing economic narrative in the United States has often extolled free markets, policymaking itself has always been more pragmatic. From its very founding, the US has had a hybrid model in which the state directs, subsidizes, and occasionally bails out private enterprise in the service of national priorities.
snippet: While the prevailing economic narrative in the United States has often extolled free markets, policymaking itself has always been more pragmatic. From its very founding, the US has had a hybrid model
creator: Shang-Jin Wei
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Three Essentials of a More Equal World Order
content: A growing awareness of the inequities embedded in the world order has prompted calls for reform. Building a multilateral system that serves the global majority will require redesigning the international financial architecture, especially in relation to sovereign debt and corporate taxation.

creator: Jenny Ricks
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The US as the World’s Robber Baron
content: Like the Gilded Age robber barons, the United States under President Donald Trump is using trade policy to benefit itself at the expense of all other countries. But now that administration surrogates are openly acknowledging the strategy, all other major trading powers will surely respond accordingly.

creator: Dani Rodrik
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A Democratic Opening for Azerbaijan
content: With Armenia's recent elections likely to usher in an era of greater stability—and less Russian influence—in the South Caucasus, neighboring Azerbaijan is well-positioned to deepen cooperation with the US and Europe. But without appropriate conditions, new strategic partnerships with the West could fail to deliver for Azerbaijanis.

creator: Ali Karimli
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Mismeasure of Europe’s Economy
content: While Nobel laureate economists Paul Krugman and Philippe Aghion disagree over how to measure Europe’s productivity gap with the United States, both overlook the value of its accumulated assets. That blind spot obscures the real story: Europe is living off a remarkable inheritance it has yet to convert into new growth.

creator: Sami Mahroum
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Reinventing Development Finance
content: Faced with shrinking concessional finance and rising borrowing costs, Rwanda has worked with the World Bank Group to leverage limited public funds and risk-sharing instruments to unlock private capital at scale and on affordable terms. It's an arrangement holding important lessons for low-income countries that must do more with less.

creator: Yusuf Murangwa
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Mamdani’s American Dream
content: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Independence Day address was a perfect example of ideology in the positive sense of the term. It overturned both the right-wing populist narrative of the United States and the left-wing "woke" critique with a vision that has the potential to mobilize a majority.

creator: Slavoj Žižek
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Vulnerable Are US Financial Markets?
content: Fueled by more than $1 trillion in borrowed money and rising leverage across the financial system, US stock markets may have entered bubble territory. Two developments could trigger a sharp correction: higher interest rates and a loss of confidence in the handful of tech giants driving the AI boom.
snippet: Fueled by more than $1 trillion in borrowed money and rising leverage across the financial system, US stock markets may have entered bubble territory. Two developments could trigger a sharp
creator: Dambisa Moyo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Concessional Finance Is Needed More Than Ever
content: As debt burdens rise and climate shocks intensify, sustainable financing mechanisms are becoming indispensable. The Islamic Development Bank’s new concessional fund offers a promising model for mobilizing the resources needed to protect development gains and invest in resilience.
snippet: As debt burdens rise and climate shocks intensify, sustainable financing mechanisms are becoming indispensable. The Islamic Development Bank’s new concessional fund offers a promising model for
creator: Muhammad Al Jasser
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Europe Must Face the World on Its Own
content: Under pressure from Russian aggression—which has reached its full expression in the ongoing war against Ukraine—and an increasingly hostile US administration, Europe urgently needs to make progress toward a defense union. But that will require Franco-German leadership of the kind that is not yet on offer.
